articles.title_en: Russian social network VK removes diabetes mutual aid section at internet regulator’s request
articles.lead_en: The Russian social network VK removed the mutual aid section from a diabetes community called “Nyura Sharikova. Sakharny Diabet” at Roskomnadzor’s request, the project’s founder Nyura Sharikova announced on April 21.

articles.title_en: Russia’s Investigative Committee orders review of children’s author Grigory Oster’s poetry collection
articles.lead_en: Alexander Bastrykin, the head of Russia’s Investigative Committee, has ordered a review of books by children’s author Grigory Oster over content that raises “pedagogical concerns,” the agency’s press service said on April 21.

articles.title_en: Teenager in Russia injured after picking up explosive device disguised as bundle of banknotes
articles.lead_en: A teenager in Perm sustained burns after picking up a device disguised as a bundle of banknotes that detonated when he lifted it off the ground, Perm Krai’s Territorial Security Ministry announced, according to the Russian news agency Interfax on April 21.

articles.title_en: Russia’s state-backed messaging app Max renamed ’Макс’ (Maks) in app stores amid law restricting foreign loanwords
articles.lead_en: Russia’s state-backed messaging app Max has been renamed in app stores, where it now appears as ”Макс” (Maks) on both the App Store and Google Play.

articles.title_en: Zelensky says Ukraine has completed repairs on Druzhba pipeline, which carries Russian oil to Europe
articles.lead_en: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky said on April 21 that specialists had completed repairs on a section of the Druzhba pipeline that Kyiv says was damaged in a Russian military strike.

articles.title_en: Russian man fined for posting photo of cat that resembles Hitler
articles.lead_en: A district court in Sochi has fined a local resident 1,000 rubles on an administrative charge of publicly displaying Nazi symbols. The case was flagged by the independent Russian news outlet Verstka and the independent Russian news outlet Mediazona.

articles.lead_en: Russian law enforcement officers in Moscow searched the homes of executives at the publishing house Eksmo, the pro-Kremlin TV channel REN TV reported, without citing a source.

articles.lead_en: The court statistics had been used to analyze trends in cases such as treason, the dissemination of “fake news” about the army, “discrediting” the military, sabotage and the justification of terrorism.

articles.title_en: Russia Forced to Slash Oil Production After Ukrainian Strikes – Reuters
articles.lead_en: The reduction, estimated between 300,000 and 400,000 barrels per day, comes as Ukraine ramps up its strike campaign against the energy and export infrastructure that accounts for roughly a quarter of Russia’s state revenue.

articles.title_en: Report: Russia opens negligence case after pacifist blogger is found hanged in prison solitary confinement cell
articles.lead_en: Russian authorities have opened a criminal case on charges of negligence following the death of pacifist blogger Vegan Khristolub Bozhiy at a prison in the Voronezh region. He was found hanged in a solitary confinement cell, a person who knew the blogger told the Telegram news channel SotaVision and the Russian Telegram news channel Ostorozhno Novosti.

articles.title_en: Biographies of Bulgakov and Vysotsky to be labeled under Russia’s drug ‘promotion’ law
articles.lead_en: Biographical books about poet Vladimir Vysotsky and writer Mikhail Bulgakov are to be labeled under Russia’s law banning drug “propaganda,” the Russian state news agencies RIA Novosti and TASS reported.

articles.title_en: Former Ura.ru editor sentenced to 5 years in prison for buying police reports from his uncle
articles.lead_en: A court in Yekaterinburg has sentenced Denis Allayarov, a former editor at the news agency Ura.ru, to five years in prison on a bribery charge, the independent Russian news outlet Mediazona reported.
